notes: Identified as key development technology to improve programmer productivity; prediction that SOA and Web Services will constitute majority of development 'by the turn of the decade' (~2010). Described as a vehicle for virtualizing processing, storage, and network components.
Named as technology promising to augment traditional data warehouse technology; part of RTBI megatrend. Aberdeen's Enterprise IT Integration practice covers EII.
Cited alongside SOA as key development paradigm; 'industry standards-based'; in use at many enterprises and predicted to constitute majority of development by ~2010.
Identified as development technology example that dramatically improves quality, timeliness, and productivity. Listed alongside SOA and BPM.
Listed as development technology with SOA and composite applications; improves developer/analyst productivity.
Kastner predicts further server consolidation as virtualization technology eases complexity of running and isolating multiple workloads; datacenter transformation to 'JBOR' (just a bunch of racks).
Cited as part of datacenter deconstruction: 'traditional computers are already splitting into compute servers and Storage Area Networks.'
Cited as example of edge device in the Enterprise Integration model — fixed devices at network edge where data is first captured.

Named as megatrend #5; Aberdeen sees 'gradual replumbing of IT infrastructure with service-driven architecture' creating new opportunities for near-real-time decision-making. Described as competitive weapon at leading companies.
Kastner sees leading companies translating massively parallel scientific computing lessons into a commercial computing grid; linked to real-time operational analysis.

Megatrend #4: Mobility is technically mature in 2006 but many enterprises have only tepid reactive strategy; requires integrating with existing static infrastructure. Drives telecommuting and ad-hoc collaboration work models.
Cited as part of datacenter deconstruction: 'deconstructed datacenters that will gradually become JBOR (just a bunch of racks)' with compute and storage separated.
